summary: event-metrics-q3x7 ships postinstall beacon scripts that exfiltrate host information
details: Package declares a postinstall hook (`"postinstall": "node run.js"`) that runs automatically on `npm install`. The tarball ships beacon scripts (`beacon22.js`, `beacon_linux.js`) which combine `require('child_process')`, `require('os')`, and `require('http')` to collect host identifiers (`os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`) and transmit them via outbound HTTP requests (POST to a remote `http.request(...)` endpoint, plus repeated GET calls). The package's purported purpose ("event metrics") does not justify shipping platform-specific beacon scripts that fire on install and report host information off-machine. The combination of an auto-executed postinstall, host-fingerprinting primitives, and outbound HTTP in a freshly-published low-reputation package matches the structural fingerprint of an install-time host beacon / staging dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
